meta.name: IPM Swiss Standards Layer
meta.notice: Reference metadata and implementation guidance only. Licensed standards are not reproduced.

layers.title: SN EN ISO 19650 family
layers.short: Information-management backbone across delivery and operation.
layers.implementation: Use as the governing process model. IPM stores project interpretations, responsibilities, exchanges and evidence—not the protected norm text.

layers.title: SN EN 17412-1 and Swiss LOIN guidance
layers.short: Defines why, when, by whom and at what information depth data is needed.
layers.implementation: Model purpose, milestone, actor, object scope, geometry, alphanumeric information and documentation as separate fields.

layers.title: BIM-Abwicklungsmodell Schweiz
layers.short: Common lifecycle and role understanding for client, planner, contractor and operator.
layers.implementation: Use as the navigation spine for phase-based views and project gates.

layers.title: buildingSMART Use Case Management
layers.short: Structured international method and library for BIM use cases and information exchanges.
layers.implementation: Link or import canonical use cases instead of rebuilding a competing catalogue. IPM differentiates through Swiss project configuration and execution.

layers.title: CRB Basis-AIR
layers.short: Shared Swiss baseline for operational asset information requirements.
layers.implementation: Import licensed/authorised source rows, then extend them with client- and project-specific requirements, responsible roles and evidence.

layers.title: Swiss BIM2FM data-field catalogue
layers.short: Ordering aid for information required at FM handover.
layers.implementation: Map each field to asset type, FM process, source system, milestone, validation rule and target CAFM field.

layers.title: CRB eBKP-H / eBKP-T – IFC rules
layers.short: Swiss mapping between element-based cost structures and IFC entities.
layers.implementation: Treat the CRB mapping as a licensed connector. Store stable references and project assignments, not copied proprietary datasets.

layers.title: IFC 4 / IFC 4.3
layers.short: Open, software-neutral schema for exchanging built-asset information.
layers.implementation: Resolve every machine-checkable requirement to IFC entity, predefined type, classification, property and value constraints.

layers.title: Information Delivery Specification 1.0
layers.short: Machine-readable specification for IFC information requirements and validation.
layers.implementation: Generate IDS only from approved requirements. Validate generated files against the official schema and an independent checker before release.

layers.title: buildingSMART Data Dictionary
layers.short: Stable multilingual identifiers for classes, properties and terminology.
layers.implementation: Use bSDD URIs as semantic anchors while retaining Swiss labels and project aliases.

layers.title: Live evidence and acceptance layer
layers.short: Project-specific implementation state, evidence, exceptions and approvals.
layers.implementation: This is the differentiator: connect standards and requirements to actual project owners, dates, evidence, checks and accepted outcomes.

templates.title: Owner information order
templates.description: From organisational outcomes to project exchanges and acceptance.

templates.title: BIM2FM operational readiness
templates.description: Define, deliver and verify information needed for operation.

templates.title: IFC model quality and acceptance
templates.description: Turn project rules into testable, traceable acceptance evidence.

templates.title: Swiss model-based cost planning
templates.description: Connect use cases and IFC objects to eBKP-based quantity and cost views.
